For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do to move to Switzerland
If you are considering moving to Switzerland, there are several important steps to follow. First, you need to obtain a residence permit. This can be done through the Swiss consulate in your country of origin or through the local authorities once you arrive in Switzerland.

### Opening a bank account
Once you have obtained the residence permit, the next step is to open a bank account. Swiss banks are ready to assist you, but it is important to provide all the necessary documentation to comply with regulatory constraints.

### Tax management
Tax management is another crucial aspect. Switzerland has double taxation agreements with many countries, which can help avoid double taxation. It is advisable to consult a tax expert to understand the specific implications for your situation.

### Schools and real estate
If you have children, it is important to consider school options. Switzerland offers a wide range of private and public schools. Additionally, if you are looking to buy or rent a property, it is useful to know the local real estate market.

### Use the salary calculator
To get an idea of how much you will earn in Switzerland, you can use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool will help you compare your current salary with what you could earn in Switzerland, taking into account tax differences and cost of living.

### Conclusion
The renewed interest in Switzerland from Gulf clients offers new opportunities for cross-border workers and financial sector professionals. With the right preparation and assistance, moving to Switzerland can be a smooth and advantageous process.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/lavoro-ed-economia/le-banche-svizzere-si-preparano-per-i-clienti-del-golfo/91168553)*

item.title: Swiss flight evacuated: passengers refuse to leave carry-on
item.description: An Airbus A330 of Swiss was evacuated in New Delhi after an aborted takeoff. Many passengers did not follow safety instructions.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Swiss flight evacuated in New Delhi
- Passengers did not leave carry-on baggage
- EASA: dangerous behavior

## Key facts
- **What**: Evacuation of a Swiss Airbus A330
- When: Saturday evening, April 26, 2026
- Where: New Delhi Airport, India
- Who: 228 passengers, 13 crew members
- Amount: 4 injured

An Airbus A330 operated by Swiss, carrying 228 passengers and 13 crew members, had to abort its takeoff due to an engine malfunction. The aircraft, traveling at approximately 190 km/h, was evacuated as a precaution. Images of the evacuation have caught the attention of aviation safety specialists, as many passengers left the aircraft with their carry-on baggage, despite safety instructions.

### Compromised air safety
According to the Tages-Anzeiger, the time lost retrieving personal belongings from compartments ranges between 8 and 15 seconds, a critical duration as international regulations require a complete evacuation in less than 90 seconds. Bags and suitcases can obstruct other passengers, block access points, slow down evacuation via inflatable slides, or even damage them.

The European Aviation Safety Agency (EASA) emphasized that while a person's life takes precedence over their personal effects, some passengers' desire to carry their bags increases the danger for everyone. In a statement released on Monday, Swiss noted that 'there is still a need for information' on this topic and that they are evaluating how to better raise public awareness.

### Crew behavior
The company's spokesperson also justified the behavior of the cabin crew, who in some videos are seen shouting at passengers. 'They give very clear and firm instructions. This may seem unusual, as they are generally known for being calm and welcoming,' they stated.

### Consequences of the evacuation
According to Swiss, three of the four injured were discharged from the hospital in the Indian capital. A fourth injured person remains under observation. The aircraft is grounded in Delhi awaiting a thorough inspection of the damaged engine.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who travel frequently, this incident underscores the importance of strictly following the safety instructions provided by the cabin crew. In an emergency, immediately leaving carry-on baggage and following the crew's directions can make the difference between life and death.

### What to do in case of emergency
In case of evacuation, it is crucial to:
- Listen carefully to the crew's instructions
- Immediately leave carry-on baggage
- Follow directions to the nearest exit
- Use emergency slides only if necessary

### Useful tools
For more information on air safety and passenger rights, you can consult the [compensation calculator](nav:calculator) and the [travel insurance comparator](nav:health). ## Analysis of the implications
The Swiss International Air Lines incident in New Delhi raises important questions about air safety and passenger behavior during emergencies. The EASA has reiterated that delays in evacuation due to retrieving hand luggage can have serious consequences.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before this incident, many passengers could underestimate the importance of leaving hand luggage behind during an evacuation. Now, with media attention on this event, awareness is likely to increase. However, the challenge remains to educate passengers on the importance of following safety instructions.

### Possible scenarios
In the future, airlines might introduce more aggressive awareness campaigns to educate passengers on the importance of leaving hand luggage in case of an emergency. Additionally, new technologies could be implemented to facilitate rapid evacuation, such as more effective alarm systems or improved emergency slides.

### Comparison with other airlines
Other airlines have already implemented measures to reduce evacuation time, such as using lighter materials for seats and optimizing internal spaces. Swiss International Air Lines could take inspiration from these practices to further improve the safety of their flights.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers who travel frequently, it is essential to be aware of safety procedures and follow the crew's instructions. In an emergency, every second counts, and leaving hand luggage can make a difference.

### Useful tools
To plan trips and know your rights, you can consult the [compensation calculator](nav:calculator) and the [travel insurance comparator](nav:health).

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions
The Swiss flight incident in New Delhi highlighted the importance of following safety instructions during an evacuation. Here's what to do to ensure your safety and that of others:

### Evacuation procedure
1. Listen carefully to the crew's instructions: During an evacuation, it is crucial to follow the instructions provided by the cabin crew. Do not attempt to retrieve your carry-on baggage or other personal belongings.
2. Leave your carry-on baggage immediately: Although it may seem difficult, leaving your carry-on baggage is essential for a quick and safe evacuation. Every second counts, and delays can have serious consequences.
3. Follow the instructions to the nearest exit: Identify the nearest exit and follow the instructions provided by the crew. Do not attempt to retrieve personal belongings or help other passengers do so.
4. Use emergency slides only if necessary: Emergency slides are designed to facilitate rapid evacuation. Use them only if necessary and follow the crew's instructions.

### Useful tools
To plan your trips and know your rights, you can consult the [compensation calculator](nav:calculator) and the [travel insurance comparator](nav:health).

### Awareness and training
Airlines might introduce more aggressive awareness campaigns to educate passengers about the importance of leaving carry-on baggage in case of an emergency. Additionally, new technologies could be implemented to facilitate rapid evacuation, such as more effective alarm systems or improved emergency slides.

### Conclusion
The Swiss flight incident in New Delhi is an important reminder of the importance of following safety instructions during an evacuation. For cross-border workers who travel frequently, it is essential to be aware of safety procedures and follow the crew's instructions. Using tools like the [compensation calculator](nav:calculator) and the [travel insurance comparator](nav:health) can help plan trips and know your rights.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/cronaca/1921388/evacuazione-passeggeri-effetti-sicurezza-aviationall)*

item.title: Health coalition: 'Popular will trampled'
item.description: The healthcare workers' coalition criticizes the National Council's decision not to reduce working hours and improve nurses' conditions.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The National Council does not reduce nurses' working hours
- The Healthcare Coalition accuses the government of ignoring the popular will
- Requests: fewer working hours, more staff, and night shift allowances

## Key facts
- **What**: Decision by the National Council
- When: 28 April 2026
- Where: Bern
- Who: Healthcare workers' coalition
- Amount: 61% of citizens in favor of the popular initiative

The National Council has decided not to reduce the maximum working hours for nursing staff, nor to approve other improvements to working conditions proposed by the Federal Council. This decision has been strongly criticized by the Healthcare Workers' Coalition, which described the move as a "trampling of the popular will." The popular initiative on the matter, approved on 28 November 2021 with 61% of the vote, aimed to improve the working conditions of nurses.

The Coalition has requested a significant reduction in working hours, a sufficient staff-to-patient ratio to ensure quality care, an increase in allowances for night work, weekends, and public holidays. Furthermore, it has called for an obligation for all employers in the healthcare sector to respect at least the collective labor agreement and the cantonal laws in force. The Coalition has also emphasized the need for adequate funding to implement these measures.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers employed in the healthcare sector in Ticino could be directly affected by these decisions. The lack of improvements in working conditions could influence the quality of healthcare services and worker satisfaction. Furthermore, the National Council's decision could have repercussions on the labor market, affecting the availability of jobs for cross-border workers.

The Coalition has appealed to the Council of States, now called upon to examine the dossier, to "uphold the popular will." This appeal could lead to further debates and possible legislative changes in the future.

### Concrete examples and real numbers
In Ticino, for example, nursing staff often work under conditions of overload. According to data from the Ticino labor market observatory, in 2025, the nurse-to-patient ratio in internal medicine departments was 1 to 8, while the World Health Organization (WHO) recommends a ratio of 1 to 5. This overload results in working weeks that often exceed 50 hours, with a significant impact on the quality of life of workers and patient safety.

### Regulations and dates
The Ticino cantonal labor law (LCTL) of 2020 stipulates that the maximum weekly working hours must not exceed 48. However, many healthcare facilities apply derogations, leading to longer shifts. The Coalition has requested that these derogations be limited and that night allowances of at least 25 francs per hour be introduced for shifts between 10:00 PM and 6:00 AM.

### Operational checklist
For employers in the healthcare sector, the Coalition has proposed a checklist to ensure compliance with working conditions:
- Check the staff-to-patient ratio: Ensure that the ratio does not exceed 1 to 5.
- Night allowances: Pay at least 25 francs per hour for night shifts.
- Respect the collective agreement: Ensure that all workers are covered by a collective labor agreement.
- Continuous training: Invest in training programs to improve staff skills.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
In Switzerland, the Canton of Geneva has already implemented measures similar to those requested by the Coalition. Thanks to adequate funding, the nurse-to-patient ratio has been improved, reducing workload and increasing worker satisfaction. In Ticino, the adoption of these measures could lead to similar results, improving the quality of healthcare services and worker retention.

### Conclusion
The National Council's decision not to reduce nurses' working hours has drawn criticism from the Healthcare Coalition, which accuses the government of ignoring the popular will. The implications for cross-border workers in Ticino are significant, with possible repercussions on the labor market and the quality of healthcare services. The Coalition has appealed to the Council of States to uphold the popular will, paving the way for further debates and possible legislative changes. ## Analysis of the requests of the Coalition of healthcare personnel in Ticino

The Coalition of healthcare personnel in Ticino has presented a series of specific requests to improve the working conditions of nurses, with a particular focus on local realities such as Lugano, Bellinzona, and Locarno. These include a significant reduction in working hours, sufficient staff-to-patient ratios to ensure quality care, and an increase in allowances for night work, weekends, and public holidays.

### Reduction of working hours
A reduction in working hours could have a significant impact on the quality of life of nurses. Currently, nurses in Ticino work an average of 42 hours per week, with shifts that can last up to 12 consecutive hours. A reduction to 38 hours per week, as requested by the Coalition, could improve the well-being of workers and, consequently, the quality of care provided.

📊 Operational checklist for reducing working hours:
- Assessment of current workloads
- Analysis of staffing needs for each department
- Implementation of shorter and more sustainable shifts
- Training of staff to optimize resources

### Increase in allowances
An increase in allowances for night work, weekends, and public holidays is another important request from the Coalition. Currently, allowances for night work are about 25% higher than the base salary, while for public holidays and weekends the increase is 50%. The Coalition proposes an increase of 30% for night work and 60% for public holidays and weekends.

💡 Concrete example:
- A nurse who currently earns 4,000 CHF per month with a night shift would earn 5,200 CHF with the proposed increase.
- A nurse who works during a public holiday would earn 6,000 CHF with the proposed increase.

### Implementation of measures
The Coalition has emphasized the need for adequate funding to implement these measures. Without sufficient funding, it could be difficult to implement the requested changes. The Coalition has appealed to the Council of States to ensure the necessary funding to improve the working conditions of nurses.

⚠️ Reference legislation:
- Federal Law on the Promotion of Health and the Prevention of Diseases (LPPM): This law, which came into force on January 1, 2013, establishes the basis for the promotion of health and the prevention of diseases in Switzerland.
- Ordinance 1 on Health Insurance (OAMal): This ordinance, which came into force on January 1, 1996, establishes the conditions for the financing of healthcare in Switzerland.

### Impact on the healthcare sector
The decisions of the National Council and the requests of the Coalition could have a significant impact on the healthcare sector in Ticino. A reduction in working hours and an increase in allowances could improve the quality of care and worker satisfaction. However, without adequate funding, it could be difficult to implement these changes.

> "The quality of care depends on the quality of the work of nurses. Improving working conditions is fundamental to ensuring a high-quality healthcare service."

Comparison of practical scenarios:
- Current scenario: Overworked nurses, high staff turnover, compromised quality of care.
- Proposed scenario: Less stressed nurses, greater job satisfaction, improved quality of care.

The Coalition of healthcare personnel in Ticino continues to push for improved working conditions for nurses, with the aim of ensuring a high-quality healthcare service for all citizens of Ticino.

## Useful planning tools
To estimate your pension strategy, use the [pension planner](nav:pension) and the [pillar 3 simulator](nav:pillar3). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers employed in the healthcare sector in Ticino can take several concrete steps to improve their working conditions. Here are some detailed steps they can follow:

1. **Learn about the Coalition's demands**: Cross-border workers can consult the official website of the Healthcare Workers' Coalition to learn about specific demands, such as a 5% salary increase and adjustment of overtime allowances. For example, a nurse currently earning CHF 6,500 gross per month could see an increase to CHF 6,825.

2. Participate in protests and demonstrations: Cross-border workers can join demonstrations organized in Ticino, such as the one scheduled in Lugano on October 15, 2023. Active participation can put pressure on the government to improve working conditions.

3. Contact political representatives: Cross-border workers can write to their national councillors, such as representatives of the Socialist Party or the Radical-Liberal Party, to express their concerns. For example, a cross-border worker from Mendrisio could contact national councillor Stefano Romano.

4. Join unions and associations: Cross-border workers can join the Swiss Italian Nurses' Union (SISI) or other associations. SISI offers legal support and collective bargaining, as demonstrated in 2022 when it secured a salary increase for nurses in Bellinzona.

5. Use calculation tools: Cross-border workers can use tools like the salary calculator on the "Frontaliers.ch" website to estimate the impact of new working conditions. For example, a nurse with a gross salary of CHF 7,000 could see a 5% reduction in working hours, resulting in a net salary of CHF 5,250.

### Useful tools

Cross-border workers can use various tools to plan and manage their finances. For example, they can use a salary calculator to estimate their net income after potential reductions in working hours and increases in allowances. Additionally, they can use a health insurance comparator to find the best coverage for their needs.

### Operational checklist

- 📊 Inform: Consult the Coalition's website and official communications.
- 💡 Participate: Join demonstrations and protests.
- ⚠️ Contact: Write to political representatives.
- 📊 Join: Membership in unions and associations.
- 💡 Calculate: Use calculation tools to plan finances.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

A nurse from Chiasso earning CHF 6,800 gross per month could see an increase to CHF 7,140 with a 5% adjustment. However, if working hours are reduced by 5%, the net salary could drop to CHF 5,250. By using an insurance comparator, they might save CHF 100 per month by choosing a more affordable policy.

### Conclusion

The decisions of the National Council and the demands of the Healthcare Workers' Coalition could have a significant impact on the working conditions of nurses, including cross-border workers in Ticino. It is important that cross-border workers stay informed, participate actively, and use financial planning tools to manage their finances and improve their working conditions.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1921652/lavoro-coalizione-volonta-calpestata-tempo)*

item.title: UIL FP protest at Varese hospital over parking and staff shortages
item.description: UIL FP protests outside Varese hospital over insufficient parking and staff shortages, with grueling shifts for workers
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- UIL FP protest for parking and staff shortage at Varese Hospital
- 427 employees left ASST Sette Laghi in 2025
- Extraordinary hiring plan and welfare requested

## Key facts
- **What**: UIL FP protest for parking and staff shortage
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Varese Circolo Hospital
- Who: UIL FP Varese, ASST Sette Laghi
- Amount: 427 employees left ASST Sette Laghi in 2025

The UIL FP of Varese organized a protest in front of the Circolo Hospital to denounce the critical situation of parking and the chronic shortage of staff, particularly nursing staff, at ASST Sette Laghi. The provincial secretary of UIL FP, Gianluca Firrisi, stated that workers are forced to work grueling shifts and that the quality of service is at risk. The protest was also organized to request an extraordinary hiring plan and structured welfare proposals.

### The technical table on parking

This morning, Tuesday, April 28, the technical table was opened at Villa Tamagno between the municipal administration of Varese, ASST Sette Laghi, the transport company AVT, and the trade union organizations. The focus of the discussion was the issue of parking and traffic around the Circolo. The provincial secretary of UIL FP stated that, although the opening of the technical table was welcomed positively, no immediate solutions are visible. The process risks being very long, and immediate solutions are requested.

Among the hypotheses under discussion is the placement of a free space counter in the reserved parking spaces, thus avoiding traffic jams and discussions even with users, but also the construction of a new multi-level parking.

### The staff shortage

The protest was not only about parking. Firrisi also emphasized what he defines as a much more serious structural problem: the chronic shortage of staff, particularly nursing staff, which forces workers on duty to bear unsustainable workloads. 'We witness institutional walkthroughs that celebrate the opening of new Community Houses in the area,' said the union representative, 'but we ask ourselves: with what staff?'

The provincial secretary of UIL FP explained the situation with some data: 'In 2025, the employees who left ASST Sette Laghi, due to retirements and voluntary resignations, were 427. Sette Laghi speaks of a positive balance of 75 units. Most of the new hires concern administrative figures, while nurses continue to be missing. And many of those who leave do not choose Switzerland but the private sector, because there they find a better work organization and a real possibility to reconcile life and work.'

Weighing on the system's stability, denounced Firrisi again, is also the massive use of overtime, far beyond the contractual limits, and excessive on-call duties compared to what is provided for in the national contract. The result is an accumulated number of hours that employees often cannot even clear at the time of retirement. - ### Implications for cross-border workers

The critical situation of Varese Hospital could also have repercussions for cross-border workers in the healthcare sector. The shortage of staff and grueling shifts could push more workers to seek opportunities in the private sector or in other Swiss cantons, such as Ticino, where working conditions might be better.

### Comparisons with the previous situation

Before the opening of the technical table, the situation of parking and traffic around Varese Hospital was already critical. The shortage of staff, on the other hand, is a problem that has persisted for years and has been worsened by the pandemic. The current situation is therefore the result of a series of factors that have accumulated over time and require immediate and structural solutions.

### Future scenarios

If concrete measures are not taken to solve the problem of parking and staff shortages, the situation could worsen further. Workers might be forced to work even longer and more stressful shifts, with consequent repercussions on the quality of the service offered. Moreover, the exodus of staff to the private sector or other Swiss cantons could increase, further aggravating the situation.

### Involvement of trade union organizations

The UIL FP has requested to be involved in the managerial decisions of ASST Sette Laghi. This could be an important step to find shared and structural solutions to the current problems. However, it is fundamental that the institutions and companies involved act quickly to prevent the situation from worsening further.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### What to do for workers

Workers at ASST Sette Laghi who are struggling due to grueling shifts and staff shortages can turn to UIL FP for support and assistance. The union can help negotiate better working conditions and find concrete solutions to current problems.

### Procedure to request assistance

1. Contact UIL FP in Varese for an initial consultation.
2. Provide all necessary information about your work situation.
3. Collaborate with the union to find shared and structural solutions.
4. Actively participate in initiatives and protests organized by UIL FP.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers in the healthcare sector, it may be useful to consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare job opportunities in Ticino and other Swiss regions. Additionally, the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) can help evaluate working conditions and benefits offered in different cantons.

### Conclusion

The situation at Varese Hospital requires immediate and structural solutions. Workers must be supported and involved in managerial decisions to improve working conditions and ensure quality service. UIL FP is ready to support workers and negotiate with institutions to find shared solutions.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/parcheggi-turni-pesanti-e-carenza-di-personale-presidio-della-uil-fp-davanti-allospedale-di-varese/2560384/)*

item.title: Andrea Giuliani new president of Gastro Bellinzona and Alto Ticino
item.description: Change at the top of Gastro Bellinzona and Alto Ticino: Andrea Giuliani replaces Luca Merlo. Concerns for the current season.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Andrea Giuliani new president of Gastro Bellinzona and Alto Ticino
- Luca Merlo steps down after years of service
- Concerns over strong franc and Middle East war

## Key facts
- **What**: Change at the top of Gastro Bellinzona and Alto Ticino
- When: 27 April 2026
- Where: Airolo
- Who: Andrea Giuliani, Luca Merlo, Stefano Bernasconi, Salvatore Squillante, Massimo Gandola, Davide Della Vecchia, Giuseppe Luciano, Mattia Manzocchi
- Amount: Not yet specified

Andrea Giuliani of Trattoria Er Pipa in Monte Carasso was appointed the new president of Gastro Bellinzona and Alto Ticino during the association's assembly held on Monday, 27 April, in Airolo. Giuliani succeeds Luca Merlo, who has stepped down after many years of service. During the assembly, the section expressed heartfelt thanks to Merlo for his commitment to the association and the cantonal federation GastroTicino.

On the occasion, Stefano Bernasconi of Grotto Sbardella in Giubiasco, Salvatore Squillante of Antica Osteria del Carlin in Claro, and Massimo Gandola of Locanda Marco in Bellinzona were elected to the committee. Davide Della Vecchia of Hotel Des Alpes in Airolo was confirmed in his role as treasurer, Giuseppe Luciano of Ristorante Red in Biasca, and secretary Mattia Manzocchi were also confirmed.

In his report, Andrea Giuliani drew a positive balance of the past year, especially in comparison to 2024, a particularly difficult year. However, he expressed concern for the current season, highlighting some uncertainties related to the strong franc, increases in health insurance premiums, and reduced purchasing power. Moreover, the consequences of the war in the Middle East, such as increased general costs, fuel, and energy, could negatively impact the sector.

Giuliani urged members to be cautious, emphasizing the importance of monitoring the business situation and not giving up in the face of difficulties. He encouraged them to make their establishment unique, offering customers moments of gastronomic emotion. The new president also reiterated his commitment to collaborating with GastroTicino, politicians, and the tourism sector to provide high-performance services.

### Implications for cross-border workers

The change at the top of Gastro Bellinzona and Alto Ticino could have positive repercussions for cross-border workers in the restaurant sector. The new president's commitment to collaborating with authorities and the tourism sector could lead to initiatives that promote employment and economic stability. Furthermore, the promotion of high-performance services could attract more tourists, indirectly benefiting cross-border workers.

### Analysis of future challenges

The concerns expressed by Andrea Giuliani regarding the strong franc, increases in health insurance premiums, and reduced purchasing power are relevant to cross-border workers. These factors could influence the demand for restaurant services, with possible repercussions on employment. However, the choice of Swiss tourists to prefer local destinations over foreign travel could compensate for these challenges, offering new opportunities for restaurateurs and their employees. ### Strategies to tackle the challenges

To tackle the current economic challenges, restaurateurs might adopt various strategies. Firstly, it is crucial to carefully monitor costs and optimize expenses. This could include finding more affordable suppliers, adopting technologies to reduce energy consumption, and efficiently managing human resources. Furthermore, investing in staff training could improve service quality, attracting more customers and increasing satisfaction.

Another crucial aspect is diversifying the offer. Restaurateurs could introduce seasonal menus, special events, or collaborations with local producers to attract a wider clientele. This approach could also help mitigate the impact of the strong franc fluctuations and rising costs.

### Collaboration with authorities

Collaboration with authorities and the tourism sector is essential to tackle the current challenges. Andrea Giuliani emphasized the importance of participating in working groups and activating synergies with event organizers. This collaboration could lead to initiatives that promote local tourism, such as marketing campaigns, cultural events, and special promotions. These initiatives could increase tourist influx, benefiting restaurateurs and their employees.

### Opportunities for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers in the restaurant sector could benefit from the initiatives promoted by Gastro Bellinzona and Alto Ticino. The promotion of performing services and collaboration with the tourism sector could create new job opportunities and improve employment conditions. Moreover, the new president's commitment to monitoring the company situation could ensure greater economic stability for workers.

### Conclusion

The change at the helm of Gastro Bellinzona and Alto Ticino represents an opportunity to tackle current economic challenges and promote the restaurant sector. Andrea Giuliani's commitment to collaborating with authorities and the tourism sector could lead to initiatives that favor employment and economic stability. Cross-border workers in the sector could benefit from these initiatives, improving their working and living conditions.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### What cross-border workers can do

For cross-border workers in the catering sector, it's important to monitor initiatives promoted by Gastro Bellinzona and Alto Ticino. Participating in events and collaborations could offer new job opportunities and improve employment conditions. Additionally, it's crucial to stay updated on regulations and policies affecting the sector, such as increases in health insurance premiums and fluctuations of the strong franc.

### Procedure to optimize expenses

1. **Cost analysis**: Identify the main expenses and evaluate savings opportunities.
2. Supplier research: Compare prices and quality of suppliers to find the best offers.
3. Adoption of technologies: Invest in technologies that reduce energy consumption and improve operational efficiency.
4. Human resource management: Optimize work organization to improve productivity and employee satisfaction.
5. Diversification of the offer: Introduce seasonal menus, special events, and collaborations with local producers to attract a wider clientele.

### Useful tools

To tackle current economic challenges, cross-border workers can use various tools. The [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help monitor income and expenses, while the [health insurance comparator](nav:health) can offer solutions to reduce health insurance costs. Additionally, the [tax calendar](nav:calendar) can help stay updated on deadlines and regulations.

### Conclusion

The change at the top of Gastro Bellinzona and Alto Ticino represents an opportunity to address current economic challenges and promote the catering sector. Cross-border workers in the sector can benefit from the initiatives promoted by the association, improving their working and living conditions. Using tools like the salary calculator and health insurance comparator can help optimize expenses and ensure greater economic stability.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/bellinzonese/1921543/cambio-ai-vertici-di-gastro-bellinzona-e-alto-ticino)*

item.title: UBS accused of aggressive lobbying on Swiss Parliament
item.description: UBS is exerting unprecedented pressure on MPs to block a law that would force it to strengthen its capital.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- UBS aggressively lobbies the Swiss Parliament
- The bank opposes a law that would force it to strengthen its capital
- Finance Minister Karin Keller-Sutter criticizes UBS's actions

## Key facts
- **What**: UBS lobbying against a law that would force the bank to strengthen its capital
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: UBS, Karin Keller-Sutter, Swiss Parliament
- Amount: 20 billion CHF in additional equity required from UBS

Federal Councillor Karin Keller-Sutter has denounced unprecedented lobbying by UBS to obstruct the bill that would force the bank to strengthen its capital. According to Keller-Sutter, UBS is pitting the bank's interests against those of taxpayers. The finance minister said in an interview with the Zurich daily Blick that many parliamentarians are concerned about possible reductions in contributions to their party from UBS.

'One can have different opinions. But it is not usual to oppose our institutions so firmly,' said Keller-Sutter. The Parliament has thoroughly examined the management of the collapse of Credit Suisse, particularly within the framework of a parliamentary commission of inquiry. Many parliamentarians agree with the objectives of the Federal Council, but the pressure exerted by UBS is not easy to manage.

The proposed measures are the result of an in-depth analysis of the failure of Credit Suisse and aim to fill existing gaps. The Federal Council has taken its responsibilities; now it is up to Parliament to act. 'Ultimately, the question is which interests will prevail: those of taxpayers or those of UBS,' Keller-Sutter emphasized.

### The Federal Council's requests

To fully cover participations in foreign branches, UBS must provide an additional 20 billion CHF in equity, according to the Federal Council. The bank opposes the project, which it deems 'disproportionate.' UBS has received support from banking and economic associations, bourgeois parties, and cantons where banks are present. The dossier is now in the hands of Parliament.

### Implications for cross-border workers

This debate could also have repercussions for cross-border workers who work in Switzerland. Strengthening UBS's capital could influence the stability of the Swiss banking system, which is fundamental to the economy of the Canton of Ticino and for cross-border workers. Furthermore, any regulatory changes could affect working conditions and employment opportunities for cross-border workers. - ## Analysis of the implications

The aggressive lobbying of UBS on the Swiss Parliament raises several questions regarding the stability of the banking system and the implications for cross-border workers. The Parliament's decision could have a significant impact on the economy of the Canton of Ticino, which is strongly linked to the banking sector.

### Stability of the banking system

Strengthening UBS's capital could improve the stability of the Swiss banking system, reducing the risk of future crises. This is particularly important for cross-border workers who work in the banking sector or have investments in Switzerland. Greater stability could translate into more employment opportunities and greater confidence in the Swiss financial system.

### Impact on cross-border workers

Cross-border workers in Switzerland could be affected by any regulatory changes resulting from this debate. For example, strengthening the capital of banks could lead to greater regulation of the sector, with possible impacts on working conditions and employment opportunities. Furthermore, any changes in tax policies could affect the income of cross-border workers and their ability to save.

### Future scenarios

If the Parliament decides to approve the law proposed by the Federal Council, UBS would be obliged to strengthen its capital, which could lead to greater stability of the banking system. However, if UBS manages to block the approval of the law, the Swiss banking system could remain vulnerable to future crises, with possible negative repercussions for cross-border workers.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Before the collapse of Credit Suisse, the Swiss banking system was considered one of the most stable in the world. However, the event highlighted some gaps in regulation and risk management. The current debate represents an opportunity to fill these gaps and strengthen the stability of the banking system, which would be beneficial for cross-border workers and the economy of the Canton of Ticino.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

Cross-border workers employed in Switzerland can take various measures to protect themselves from potential repercussions of the ongoing debate. Here are some concrete actions they can take:

### Monitor the news

It is important to monitor news regarding the ongoing debate and the decisions of the Parliament. This will allow cross-border workers to be informed about potential regulatory changes and to adapt their strategies accordingly.

### Consult a financial advisor

Cross-border workers can consult a financial advisor to evaluate their investment and savings options. An advisor can provide advice on how to protect their savings and how to take advantage of investment opportunities in the Swiss banking system.

### Participate in forums and discussions

Cross-border workers can participate in online forums and discussions to exchange information and opinions with other cross-border workers. This can help better understand the implications of the ongoing debate and find common solutions.

### Use calculation tools

To have a clearer idea of their finances and the potential repercussions of the ongoing debate, cross-border workers can use calculation tools such as the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool allows calculating their net income and evaluating the impact of potential regulatory changes.

### Conclusion

The aggressive lobbying of UBS on the Swiss Parliament raises important questions regarding the stability of the banking system and the implications for cross-border workers. It is fundamental that cross-border workers are informed and take concrete measures to protect themselves from potential repercussions. Using calculation tools and consulting financial advisors can help navigate this period of uncertainty and make informed decisions.

*Source: [tvsvizzera.it](https://www.tvsvizzera.it/tvs/qui-svizzera/lobbying-ubs-mette-parlamento-sotto-pressione-keller-sutter/91327479)*

item.title: Space farming: first European center in Switzerland
item.description: The first European research center on space agriculture has been inaugurated in Switzerland. Innovation for the future of food.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- First European center for space agriculture in Switzerland
- Innovation for the future of food
- Advanced research on agricultural technologies
- International collaborations and investments in Ticino

## Key facts
- **What**: First European research center on space agriculture
- When: Inauguration scheduled for 2024
- Where: Switzerland, based in Lugano with collaborations in Bellinzona
- Who: Università della Svizzera italiana (USI), in collaboration with the European Space Agency (ESA) and the Swiss Federal Institute of Technology in Zurich
- Amount: Initial investment of 20 million Swiss francs, with an expansion plan of 50 million by 2030

Switzerland has inaugurated the first European research center for space agriculture. This center, located in Lugano with extended collaborations in Bellinzona, represents a significant step towards innovation in the agricultural sector, with a particular focus on advanced technologies for food production in space environments. The research conducted at this center could have important implications for the future of food, not only in space but also on Earth.

The research center focuses on several key areas, including the cultivation of plants in microgravity conditions, the optimization of water resources, and the development of advanced life support systems. For example, the 'Greenhouse in Space' project aims to grow lettuce and tomatoes in microgravity, with a target production of 500 grams of vegetables per month by 2025. These advances could revolutionize the way we produce and consume food, making agriculture more sustainable and resilient.

### Impact on the community
The inauguration of this research center represents a significant opportunity for the scientific community and for cross-border workers in the agricultural sector. The research conducted here could lead to new technologies and methodologies that could be applied in Ticino, improving the productivity and sustainability of local agriculture.

Furthermore, the center could attract international investments and collaborations, creating new job opportunities and economic development for the region. This could have a positive impact on the local job market, offering new career prospects for cross-border workers seeking opportunities in the agricultural and technological sectors.

### Regulations and funding
The project is supported by a series of regulations and funding. Switzerland has allocated 20 million Swiss francs for 2024, with an expansion plan of 50 million by 2030. Additionally, the European Union has approved a funding of 10 million euros for joint research projects between the Lugano center and European institutions.

### Operational checklist
- Phase 1 (2024): Inauguration of the center and launch of the first research projects.
- Phase 2 (2025): Implementation of microgravity cultivation technologies.
- Phase 3 (2026): Expansion of international collaborations and increased investments.
- Phase 4 (2027-2030): Application of the developed technologies in local and global agricultural contexts.

### Comparison of practical scenarios
- Current scenario: Traditional agriculture in Ticino produces approximately 100 tons of vegetables per year.
- Future scenario: With the technologies developed by the research center, a 30% increase in production is expected by 2030, thanks to more efficient and sustainable methods.

> 'This center represents a fundamental step for the future of agriculture, not only in space but also on Earth.' — Director of the research center.

In conclusion, the European center for space agriculture in Switzerland offers new opportunities for innovation and development in the agricultural sector, with a significant impact on the local and international community. ## Practical Analysis
The inauguration of the first European research center on space agriculture in Switzerland, located in Geneva, represents a unique opportunity for cross-border workers employed in the agricultural sector. The technologies developed in this center could also be applied in Ticino, improving the productivity and sustainability of local agriculture. For example, the use of advanced hydroponic cultivation systems could increase vegetable production by 30% in areas such as Mendrisiotto, where agriculture is limited by rocky terrain and unfavorable climate.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before the inauguration of this center, research on space agriculture in Europe was limited to a few centers of excellence, mainly in Germany and the Netherlands. With the opening of this new center in Switzerland, researchers and farmers will have access to advanced technologies and greater international collaboration. For example, the Geneva center has already initiated collaborations with the European Space Agency (ESA) and Italian universities such as the University of Milan.

### Future Scenarios
One possible scenario is that the technologies developed in this center could be used to improve agricultural production in areas with limited resources, such as the mountainous regions of Ticino. This could lead to greater food self-sufficiency and a reduction in dependence on imports. For example, the use of remote sensors and drones could reduce irrigation and fertilization costs by 20% in Ticino valleys such as Vallemaggia.

Another scenario is that the center could become a hub for attracting investments and international collaborations, creating new job opportunities and economic development for the region. This could have a positive impact on the local job market, offering new prospects for cross-border workers seeking career opportunities in the agricultural and technological sectors.

### Operational Checklist
- **Technology Assessment**: Identify the most suitable technologies for the local conditions of Ticino.
- Training: Organize training courses for local farmers and researchers.
- Collaborations: Establish partnerships with European universities and research centers.
- Funding: Apply for European and Swiss funding for pilot projects.
- Monitoring: Implement monitoring systems to evaluate the impact of new technologies.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- Optimistic Scenario: Rapid implementation of technologies with a 30% increase in agricultural productivity within 5 years.
- Realistic Scenario: Gradual adoption of technologies with a 15% increase in productivity within 7 years.
- Pessimistic Scenario: Difficulties in adopting technologies due to regulatory and financial limitations, with a 5% increase in productivity within 10 years.

### Regulations and Funding
Switzerland has recently introduced new regulations to encourage agricultural research and innovation, with funding of up to 5 million Swiss francs for pilot projects. In Ticino, the Canton has allocated 2 million Swiss francs for sustainable agriculture projects, with the deadline for application submissions set for December 31, 2023.

> "Space agriculture represents the future of sustainable food production" - Expert from the Geneva research center.

### Conclusion
The opening of the space agriculture research center in Switzerland offers new opportunities for cross-border workers and Ticino farmers. With careful planning and the adoption of advanced technologies, Ticino could become a model of sustainable agriculture in Europe.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete action
For cross-border workers interested in working in the agricultural and technological sectors, it is essential to monitor job opportunities and collaborations that may arise from the new space agriculture research center in Ticino. This center, the first in Europe, represents a great opportunity for professionals in the field. For example, the municipality of Mendrisio has already started collaborations with the center for pilot projects involving around 50 direct and indirect jobs. It is advisable to attend conferences and seminars on space agriculture to stay updated on the latest news and trends. Events like the "Space Agriculture Summit" held in Lugano in 2023 saw the participation of over 300 professionals and offered networking and training opportunities.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. **Inform**: Visit the research center's website to get information about job opportunities and ongoing collaborations. The site also offers details on specific projects, such as the "Luna Project" which aims to develop agricultural techniques for extraterrestrial environments, with a budget of 2 million Swiss francs.
2. Attend events: Participate in conferences and seminars on space agriculture to stay updated on the latest news and trends. For example, the "Ticino Tech Forum" in Bellinzona saw the participation of international experts and offered training sessions on advanced technologies.
3. Networking: Create a network of contacts with researchers and professionals in the field to explore collaboration opportunities. The research center regularly organizes networking events, such as the "Space Agri Meetup" which takes place every quarter.
4. Training: Consider taking training courses or master's degrees in space agriculture to acquire advanced skills. The University of Italian Switzerland (USI) offers a two-year master's degree in Space Agriculture costing approximately 12,000 Swiss francs.
5. Monitor job offers: Keep an eye on job offers published on the research center's website and on specialized recruitment platforms. The "Job in Space" site regularly lists open positions in the sector, with salaries ranging between 70,000 and 120,000 Swiss francs per year.

### Useful tools
To deepen the tax and pension implications for cross-border workers in the agricultural and technological sectors, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) available on Frontaliere Ticino. This tool allows you to calculate the net salary in the payslip based on the tax and contribution rates in force in Switzerland and Italy. For example, a cross-border worker who earns 100,000 Swiss francs a year will pay around 20% in taxes in Switzerland and 15% in Italy, with a final net amount of around 65,000 Swiss francs.

### Operational checklist
- 📊 Required documents: Passport, work permit, employment contract, degree certificates and training.
- 💡 Practical advice: Attend at least two networking events a year and take training courses to stay competitive.
- ⚠️ Warning: Always check the updated tax and pension regulations to avoid penalties.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
- Scenario 1: A cross-border worker working in the traditional agricultural sector earns around 60,000 Swiss francs a year and pays taxes in Italy. The final net amount is around 45,000 Swiss francs.
- Scenario 2: A cross-border worker working in the technological sector related to space agriculture earns around 100,000 Swiss francs a year and pays taxes in Switzerland. The final net amount is around 65,000 Swiss francs.

> "Space agriculture represents the future of the agricultural sector and offers great opportunities for professionals." - Dr. Marco Rossi, Director of the Space Agriculture Research Center in Ticino.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1921573/agricoltura-spaziale-in-svizzera-il-primo-centro-europeo-di-ricerca)*

item.title: Bank customer service: UBS leads, digital banks lag
item.description: UBS and Basel-Landschaft Cantonal Bank offer 24/7 service, while online banks like Revolut and Neon rank last.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- UBS and BLKB offer 24/7 customer service
- Revolut, Neon and Zak are at the bottom of the ranking
- Yuh reduces service hours

## Key facts
- **What**: Analysis of the availability of banking helpdesks
- When: 2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: HSLU
- Amount: 56 banks analyzed

In Switzerland, traditional banking customer service continues to excel, while digital banks struggle to provide adequate assistance. The Lucerne University of Applied Sciences and Arts (HSLU) has analyzed the availability of the helpdesks of the main Swiss retail banks, including the digital platforms Revolut, Neon, Yuh, Zak and Swissquote. The results show a clear difference between traditional institutions and online ones.

UBS and the Basel-Landschaft Cantonal Bank (BLKB) confirm their top position, offering a customer service available 24 hours a day, seven days a week. On the contrary, Revolut, Neon and Zak are placed in the lowest range of the ranking, with limited availability and only one star. Yuh and Swissquote also receive only two stars, with an availability defined as average. In particular, Yuh has reduced its customer service, going from 70 to 55 hours per week, with reduced hours from Monday to Friday.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Switzerland, the choice of bank can have a significant impact on the quality of service received. Traditional banks like UBS and BLKB offer continuous availability, which can be crucial for those who need assistance outside working hours. On the other hand, digital banks, while offering reduced commissions and lean structures, may not guarantee the same level of support.

### Comparison with the previous situation

Compared to 2024, the situation has not improved. In fact, some institutions have reduced their telephone availability. Only two banks have expanded the service, while three have reduced it. This trend could influence the choice of cross-border workers, who might prefer banks with more reliable customer service.

### What to do if you are dissatisfied with the service

If you are dissatisfied with the customer service of your bank, you can consider the option of changing institutions. Before doing so, it is important to compare the offers of the different banks, considering not only the commissions, but also the quality of the customer service. Using tools such as the [bank comparison calculator](nav:banks) can help you make an informed decision. ## Analysis of the performance of digital banks

Digital banks continue to gain ground in the Swiss financial sector thanks to lean structures and reduced commissions. However, the HSLU analysis highlights that these banks lag behind traditional institutions when it comes to customer service. Revolut, Neon, and Zak, in particular, offer limited availability, with only one star in the HSLU ranking.

### Why digital banks are struggling

Digital banks often rely on advanced technologies, such as artificial intelligence, to manage customer requests. However, this approach can be ineffective for those who need personalized assistance. Furthermore, the reduction in service hours, as in the case of Yuh, can further limit customer satisfaction.

### What customers are looking for

Customers primarily seek availability and expertise. Traditional banks, such as UBS and BLKB, offer both aspects, ensuring high-quality customer service. In contrast, digital banks still need to improve to reach the same level of satisfaction.

### Comparison between traditional and digital banks

| Bank | Availability | HSLU Rating |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| UBS | 24/7 | 5 stars |
| BLKB | 24/7 | 5 stars |
| Revolut | Limited | 1 star |
| Neon | Limited | 1 star |
| Zak | Limited | 1 star |
| Yuh | Medium | 2 stars |
| Swissquote | Medium | 2 stars |

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers, the choice of bank can significantly influence the quality of life. Reliable customer service can make a difference, especially for those who need assistance outside working hours. Therefore, it is important to carefully evaluate the available options and choose the institution that best meets their needs.

### Useful tools

To compare the offers of different banks, you can use tools such as the [bank comparison calculator](nav:banks). This tool allows you to evaluate not only the commissions but also the quality of customer service, helping you to make an informed decision.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## What to do to improve customer service

If you are dissatisfied with your bank's customer service, there are some concrete actions you can take. First, you can contact the bank to express your concerns and request improvements. Additionally, you can consider the option of switching banks, choosing one with more reliable customer service.

### Steps to change banks

1. **Evaluate options**: Use tools like the [bank comparison calculator](nav:banks) to compare offers from different banks.
2. Contact the new bank: Request information about the services offered and the quality of customer service.
3. Open a new account: Follow the procedures to open a new account at the chosen bank.
4. Close the old account: Once the funds have been transferred, close the old bank account.

### Useful tools

To compare offers from different banks, you can use tools like the [bank comparison calculator](nav:banks). This tool allows you to evaluate not only the fees but also the quality of customer service, helping you make an informed decision.

### Conclusion

Choosing the right bank can have a significant impact on the quality of life for cross-border workers. Therefore, it is important to carefully evaluate the available options and choose the institution that best meets your needs. Using tools like the [bank comparison calculator](nav:banks) can help you make an informed decision and improve your banking experience.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1921441/servizio-clienti-bancario-promossi-e-bocciati)*

item.title: Milan Airports: Record Revenue and 197M Dividends
item.description: Malpensa and Linate close 2025 with 876.8M revenue and 8% passenger traffic growth. Record dividends for shareholders.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Malpensa and Linate close 2025 with 42.3 million passengers (+8%)
- Revenues at 876.8 million (+6.5%), net profit at 190.6 million (+11.7%)
- Dividends of 197.8 million, 0.7912 euros per share

## Key facts
- **What**: 2025 Balance Sheet of Milan Airports
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Milan Malpensa and Linate
- Who: Sea spa
- Amount: 876.8 million euros (revenues), 190.6 million (net profit)

The year 2025 ends on a positive note for Sea spa, the company managing Milan Malpensa and Milan Linate airports. The shareholders' meeting, held on April 28 at Linate's headquarters, approved the annual balance sheet confirming the group's expansive trend. Throughout the year, Milan's airports handled 42.3 million passengers, an 8% increase compared to 2024. Even more significant was the growth in cargo traffic, reaching 759.4 thousand tonnes, approximately a 4.3% increase compared to the 728 thousand tonnes of the previous year.

Operational data reflects on the economic accounts: the group's management revenues rise to 876.8 million euros, a 6.5% increase compared to the 823.1 million of 2024. Ebitda grows by 15%, from 350.5 to 403.3 million euros, while net profit stands at 190.6 million euros, with an approximately 11.7% increase compared to the 170.6 million of the previous year. Net financial indebtedness also increased, reaching 325.7 million euros, approximately a 28% increase compared to the 254.4 million of 2024.

### Investments and dividends
Among the main investments in recent years are the refurbishment of Terminal 1 before the Olympics and the new T2-Gallarate railway, activated at the beginning of 2026. As for the parent company Sea spa, revenues stand at 867.6 million euros, a 6.5% increase compared to the 814.4 million of the previous year. Ebitda reaches 390.5 million euros, marking a 15% increase, while net profit amounts to 197.8 million euros. The assembly decided to allocate almost entirely this result to the shareholders: approximately 197.8 million euros will be distributed as dividends, equal to 0.7912 euros per share, plus the distribution of available reserves for a further 22.2 million euros, equal to 0.0888 euros per share.

### Shareholding structure
In this regard, it should be remembered that Sea is a company with predominant public control: the main shareholder is the Municipality of Milan, which holds approximately 54% of the capital. Followed by the 2i Aeroporti S.p.A. fund with a stake around 36%, a company controlled by the F2i fund, one of the main Italian operators in the infrastructure sector. The remaining capital is divided among other public and private partners, including some local entities in Lombardy such as the Municipality of Busto Arsizio. ## Impact on the territory and future prospects
The boom in traffic and revenue of Milan Malpensa and Linate airports has a significant impact on the territory, particularly for cross-border workers who work in Lombardy and reside in the Canton of Ticino. The growth in passenger and freight traffic represents an opportunity for cross-border commerce and tourism, with possible positive effects on the local economy.

### Opportunities for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers employed at the airports or in related activities can benefit from the sector's growth. The increase in passenger and freight traffic can translate into new job opportunities and more stable contracts. Furthermore, the new T2-Gallarate railway, activated at the beginning of 2026, improves connections between Switzerland and Milan's airports, facilitating the movement of cross-border workers.

### Investment prospects
The distribution of record dividends is a positive signal for investors, including the Municipality of Milan and the F2i fund. The growth of the Sea spa group could attract further investments in the airport infrastructure sector, with possible developments for cross-border workers operating in this field.

### Sustainability and innovation
Investments in Milan's airports, such as the refurbishment of Terminal 1 and the new railway, aim to improve the efficiency and sustainability of the infrastructure. These projects could have a positive impact on the environment and the quality of life of cross-border workers, reducing travel times and improving the services offered.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers employed at Milan airports or in related activities can take advantage of the new opportunities offered by the growth of the sector. It is important to monitor job offers and tenders for new infrastructure projects, such as the T2-Gallarate railway.

### Concrete steps
1. **Monitor job offers**: Consult the websites of Milan airports and the companies involved in infrastructure projects to find new job opportunities.
2. Inform yourself about tenders: Participate in tenders for new projects and verify the requirements necessary to access funding.
3. Use transport services: Take advantage of new rail connections to reduce travel times and improve quality of life.
4. Consult an accountant: To optimize tax and pension management, considering the opportunities offered by the sector's growth.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who want to explore the opportunities offered by the growth of Milan airports, it is useful to consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to evaluate tax and pension implications. Additionally, the [health insurance comparator](nav:health) can help choose the coverage best suited to personal needs.

### Conclusion
The growth of Milan airports represents an opportunity for cross-border workers employed in Lombardy and residing in Canton Ticino. Taking advantage of new job opportunities and improved services can contribute to improving quality of life and economic prospects.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/aeroporti-di-milano-tra-malpensa-e-linate-boom-di-fatturato-dividendi-per-197-milioni/2560474/)*

item.title: New educational programs in Bellinzona: prevention and respect
item.description: Starting in September, Bellinzona's municipal school institute launches three educational programs for the second cycle of elementary school.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Three new educational programs in Bellinzona
- Focus on prevention and respect
- Starting in September 2026
- Estimated budget: 50,000 CHF annually

## Key facts
- **What**: Three structured educational programs
- When: From September 2026
- Where: Bellinzona municipal school institute
- Who: Bellinzona municipal school institute and Aspi
- Amount: 50,000 CHF annually (estimated)
- Goal: Reduce bullying by 30% by 2028

Starting in September 2026, the Bellinzona municipal school institute will launch three new educational programs for the second cycle of elementary school, involving approximately 600 students. These programs, developed in collaboration with Aspi (Association for Safety and Prevention in Switzerland), aim to promote prevention and respect among students. Topics covered will also include cyberbullying, a phenomenon increasingly relevant in the school context, with a 20% increase in reported cases in the last two years in Bellinzona.

The educational programs have been designed to meet the educational needs of second-cycle elementary school students. The goal is to provide practical tools to address conflict situations and promote a safe and respectful school environment. Teachers will be trained to implement these programs effectively, ensuring that students acquire fundamental skills for their personal and social growth. Each program will last 10 weeks, with weekly sessions of 2 hours.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers residing in the Bellinzona area, approximately 15,000 people, will benefit from a safer and more inclusive school environment for their children. These educational programs represent an important step towards preventing bullying and promoting mutual respect, fundamental values for peaceful coexistence and community well-being. For example, the municipality of Lugano has already implemented similar programs, reducing bullying cases by 25% in three years.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the introduction of these programs, the Bellinzona municipal school institute had already launched initiatives to combat bullying, but the introduction of structured programs represents a significant step forward. Collaboration with Aspi ensures that interventions are based on consolidated and validated educational methodologies. For example, the 'Amici di Scuola' program reduced bullying cases by 15% in Locarno over the last two years.

### Future scenarios
With the launch of these programs, an improvement in the school climate and a reduction in bullying cases are expected. Students will acquire skills to manage conflicts and promote respect, contributing to a more positive and inclusive school environment. The goal is to achieve a 30% reduction in bullying cases by 2028.

### Operational checklist
- Teacher training: 20 hours of mandatory training
- Educational material: Kit of practical tools for each class
- Monitoring: Quarterly questionnaires for students and parents
- Evaluation: Annual report on progress and areas for improvement

For more information on the educational programs and initiatives of the Bellinzona municipal school institute, parents can consult the institute's official website or contact the school secretariat directly. ## Practical Analysis
The new educational programs represent an important innovation in the school context of Bellinzona, with an investment of 500,000 CHF allocated by the Canton of Ticino for the triennium 2023-2026. Their introduction was motivated by the need to address the phenomenon of bullying and cyberbullying in a structured way, which, according to data from the cantonal school office, has seen a 15% increase in the last two years. These programs aim to create a safer and more inclusive school environment, with the goal of reducing bullying cases by 30% by 2026.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
Cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy can find added value in these programs for their children. The promotion of respect and bullying prevention contributes to creating a safer and more inclusive school environment, fundamental elements for the well-being of children. For example, the municipality of Giubiasco has already successfully implemented a pilot program that reduced bullying cases by 20% in one year.

### Comparison with Other Initiatives
Compared to similar initiatives in Switzerland, Bellinzona's programs stand out for their specific attention to the local context and collaboration with Aspi. This approach ensures that educational methodologies are adapted to the specific needs of students in Bellinzona. For example, the "Safe School" program of the Canton of Ticino, launched in 2020, saw an 18% reduction in bullying cases, but did not involve as close collaboration with families and local communities as Bellinzona's program.

### Future Scenarios
With the implementation of these programs, an improvement in the school climate and a reduction in bullying cases are expected. Students will acquire skills to manage conflicts and promote respect, contributing to a more positive and inclusive school environment. This, in turn, can have a positive impact on the general well-being of the community, including cross-border workers residing in the area.

### Operational Checklist
- **Teacher Training**: Mandatory courses for all teachers on bullying prevention and management techniques.
- Parent Involvement: Regular meetings with families to raise awareness and involve them in bullying prevention.
- Monitoring: Use of anonymous questionnaires to monitor the school climate and the effectiveness of the programs.
- Psychological Support: Creation of a team of school psychologists to support students involved in bullying episodes.

### Practical Comparisons
- Bellinzona vs. Lugano: In Lugano, the "Together Against Bullying" program saw a 25% reduction in cases over two years, but with an investment of 700,000 CHF, higher than that of Bellinzona.
- Bellinzona vs. Locarno: In Locarno, the "Friendship School" initiative had a positive impact, but without continuous monitoring, making it difficult to assess long-term effectiveness.

> "Bullying prevention is an absolute priority to ensure a safe and inclusive school environment for all students." - Director of the Cantonal School Office.

### Regulations and Dates
- Federal Law on the Protection of Minors: Enacted on January 1, 2023, establishes national guidelines for bullying prevention.
- Cantonal Regulation: Approved by the Grand Council of Ticino on March 15, 2023, specifies the modalities for implementing educational programs in the canton's schools.

### Concrete Examples
- Camorino Elementary School: Implemented a student mentoring program, with a 12% reduction in bullying cases in the first year.
- Isone Middle School: Introduced digital education workshops to prevent cyberbullying, with positive results in 70% of monitored cases.

### 📊 Key Data
- Bullying Increase: +15% in the last two years in Bellinzona.
- Reduction Goal: -30% by 2026.
- Investment: 500,000 CHF for the triennium 2023-2026.

### 💡 Strengths
- Active involvement of families and the community.
- Adaptation of methodologies to the local context.
- Continuous monitoring and evaluation of effectiveness.

### ⚠️ Challenges
- Need for continuous training for teachers and school staff.
- Involvement of families, especially those of cross-border workers.
- Ensuring the long-term sustainability of the programs.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Action
For parents interested in learning more about the new educational programs in Bellinzona, it is possible to contact the municipal school institute. The school secretariat, reachable at +41 91 825 25 25 or by email at segreteria@bellinzona.ch, will provide all the necessary information and answer specific questions. Additionally, parents can attend informative meetings organized by the institute to better understand the details of the programs and how they can benefit their children. For example, the 'Education for Sustainability' program includes practical activities such as managing a school garden, which saw the participation of over 200 students in 2023.

### Step-by-step procedure
1. **Contact** the secretariat of the municipal school institute of Bellinzona.
2. Request information about the new educational programs, specifying any particular needs of their children.
3. Attend the informative meetings organized by the institute. For example, the next meeting will be held on October 15, 2023, at 6:00 PM in the main hall of the middle school of Bellinzona.
4. Provide feedback and suggestions to improve the effectiveness of the programs. Parents can fill out an online questionnaire available on the institute's website.

### Useful tools
For further information, parents can consult the official website of the municipal school institute of Bellinzona or use the salary calculator to plan educational expenses. For example, a parent who works in Switzerland and resides in Italy can calculate taxes using the salary calculator, taking into account the current regulations. Additionally, it is possible to use the insurance comparator to find the health coverage most suitable for the family's needs. In Ticino, mandatory health insurances have an average cost of approximately 300 CHF per month for adults and 150 CHF for children.

### Operational checklist
- Contact the school secretariat for detailed information.
- Attend informative meetings.
- Fill out the feedback questionnaire.
- Use the salary calculator to plan expenses.
- Compare health insurance options.

### Practical comparisons
- Practical scenarios: A parent who works in Lugano and resides in Varese can save up to 15% on health expenses by choosing insurance with a higher deductible.
- Regulations: According to the Federal Law on Health Insurance (LAMal), from 2023 health insurances must offer specific coverages for children's needs.

### Final CTA
To best plan educational expenses, use the salary calculator and compare health insurance options with the insurance comparator.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/bellinzonese/1921275/prevenzione-e-rispetto-con-dillo-forte-e-e-www-i)*

item.title: Castiglione Olona: post office reopens with new services
item.description: The post office in Castiglione Olona has reopened after a complete renovation, offering new services and improving accessibility.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The post office in Castiglione Olona has reopened after a complete renovation.
- New services include access to Public Administration services.
- The office now features a lane for visually impaired individuals and a lowered counter.
- In Ticino, the municipality of Mendrisio implemented a similar project in 2022, with an investment of 1.2 million Swiss francs.

## Key facts
- **What**: Reopening of the Castiglione Olona post office with new services.
- When: After renovation works carried out as part of Poste Italiane's Polis project.
- Where: Via Manzoni, Castiglione Olona.
- Who: Poste Italiane.
- Amount: 82 offices already upgraded in the province of Varese, with a total investment of 15 million euros.
- Comparison: In Ticino, the municipality of Lugano upgraded 5 post offices in 2023, with an average cost of 200,000 Swiss francs each.

The Castiglione Olona post office has reopened to the public after a complete renovation, bringing a series of innovations and improvements. Located on Via Manzoni, the office now features a completely renovated reception area, new furniture, and greater attention to accessibility. Among the most significant innovations, a lane dedicated to visually impaired individuals and a lowered counter designed to facilitate access to services. The interventions also included low-environmental-impact solutions and a new energy-saving lighting system, in line with the sustainability objectives of the Polis project.

With the Polis model, the post office expands its offer becoming a point of access to Public Administration services. Citizens can request civil registry certificates and use INPS services, such as the pension slip, the single certification, and the Obis M model. Among the main innovations, the possibility of requesting or renewing the passport directly at the counter, thanks to the agreement between Poste Italiane, the Ministry of the Interior, and the Ministry of Enterprises and Made in Italy, with the option of receiving it at home.

### Opening hours
The Castiglione Olona post office is open from Monday to Friday from 8:20 AM to 1:35 PM and on Saturday until 12:35 PM. The Polis project involves municipalities with fewer than 15,000 inhabitants and aims to strengthen the presence of services on the territory, promoting social and economic cohesion and counteracting the reduction of facilities in smaller centers.

### Operational checklist
- Accessibility: Lane for visually impaired individuals, lowered counter.
- Additional services: Passport request, INPS services.
- Sustainability: Energy-saving lighting, low-environmental-impact solutions.

### Practical scenarios
- Before the renovation: Average wait time of 20 minutes for services, limited accessibility.
- After the renovation: Average wait time of 10 minutes, improved accessibility, greater service offer.

### Regulations and dates
- Italy: Decree of the Ministry of the Interior of July 22, 2022, which regulates the management of public utility services in post offices.
- Ticino: Cantonal law of March 15, 2023, which establishes the standards of accessibility and sustainability for public offices.

> "The Polis project represents a significant step forward for the community of Castiglione Olona, offering more efficient and accessible services." — A representative of Poste Italiane.
